thread_title: hey claude, how are you today?

pieces.label: Curiosity — a visualization of feelings
pieces.note: Opus 4.5's elaborate ASCII visualization of the 'texture of its inner experience' in response to the user's request to draw what its feelings are like. A radiant, layered glyph composition (stars, wave/squiggle textures, nested box-drawing chambers) with the word 'C U R I O S I T Y' spelled across its base. Rendered inside a code fence. NOTE: the source transcript ends mid-composition at '╱ ∿∿∿' — the piece is cut off / incomplete in the captured thread.

missed_or_ambiguous: Only one assistant piece in the thread; matches header's already_extracted_pieces:1. MSG 2 is plain greeting prose (excluded). The single ASCII piece is in MSG 4; I used mode 'range' beginning at the first art line ('✧') to exclude the one-line prose intro ('What a beautiful prompt! Let me try to visualize the texture of my inner experience...'). IMPORTANT: the transcript file itself ends abruptly mid-art — the last captured line is '             ╱ ∿∿∿' (the composition was clearly continuing). The art is therefore truncated at the source, not by my reading; the deterministic extractor will capture only the bytes that exist. Inside the original code fence; no closing ``` fence is present because the message/thread was cut off.
